attractions.name: 21c Museum Hotel Louisville
attractions.description: 21c Museum Hotel Louisville is the flagship property of a unique boutique hotel chain that doubles as a 24/7 contemporary art museum. Housed in five renovated 19th-century warehouses on historic Museum Row, it features rotating exhibitions, site-specific installations, and the acclaimed Proof on Main restaurant. 21c Museum Hotels, Wikipedia
attractions.region: West Main Street Historic District / Downtown Historic Hotels of America
attractions.hours: Museum: Open 24/7; Proof on Main (Breakfast): Mon-Fri 7am-10am; Proof on Main (Brunch): Sat-Sun 8am-2pm; Proof on Main (Lunch): Mon-Fri 11am-2pm; Proof on Main (Dinner): Sun-Wed 5pm-9pm, Thu-Sat 5pm-10pm; Lounge: Sun-Wed 11am-10pm, Thu-Sat 11am-11pm Proof on Main, 21c Museum Hotels
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday 21c Museum Hotels
attractions.admission: Free (Museum galleries are free and open to the public) 21c Museum Hotels, OpenTable
highlights: '9,000 square feet of contemporary art exhibition space open 24/7 [Tripadvisor', 'Iconic 4-foot tall red penguin sculptures that roam the property Historic Hotels of America', 'Award-winning Proof on Main restaurant specializing in Ohio River Valley cuisine Proof on Main', "30-foot tall golden replica of Michelangelo's 'David' sculpture at the entrance Wikipedia", "Immersive 'Asleep In The Cyclone' artist-designed suite 21c Museum Hotels", 'Full-service spa offering massage treatments and wellness services 21c Museum Hotels']

attractions.name: Actors Theatre of Louisville
attractions.description: Actors Theatre of Louisville is a non-profit performing arts organization and the official State Theatre of Kentucky. It focuses on unlocking human potential and building community through innovative storytelling and performances that reflect the complexity of modern times.
attractions.hours: Box Office (Phone): Monday - Friday, 10 a.m. - 4 p.m. ET; Box Office (In-person): Thursdays, 10 a.m. - 2 p.m.; Performance hours typically begin 90-120 minutes before curtain time.
attractions.days_open: Monday through Friday (Box Office Phone); Thursdays (In-person Box Office); Performance days vary by production schedule.
attractions.admission: Tickets vary by production; Free Memberships are available for priority access, while paid Blue, Red, and Purple memberships waive ticket fees. Student and Educator tickets are available for $15 on Wednesday performances. Vivid Seats historical data indicates average prices range from $84 to $139.
highlights: ['Recipient of a Tony Award for Distinguished Achievement.', 'Designated as the official State Theatre of Kentucky since 1974.', 'Housed in a historic 19th-century building, the Old Bank of Louisville, which is a National Historic Landmark.', 'Features three distinct performance spaces: the Pamela Brown Auditorium, the Bingham Theater, and the Victor Jory Theater.', 'Home to the Storytelling (r)Evolution, a lab for innovation at the intersection of art and social transformation.', 'Renowned for producing groundbreaking new American plays and reviving relevant classics.']
attractions.tips: Join the free membership tier online to access the best ticket prices and receive priority booking. The theater is a card-only venue and does not accept cash payments. For the best value, look for Student & Educator nights on Wednesdays when tickets are discounted to $15.
attractions.nearby: Muhammad Ali Center, Louisville Slugger Museum & Factory, Frazier History Museum, Kentucky Science Center, Evan Williams Bourbon Experience, KFC Yum! Center

attractions.name: Against the Grain Brewery & Public House
attractions.description: Against the Grain Brewery & Public House is a pioneering craft brewery located in a historic former train station at Louisville Slugger Field. It is renowned for its Victorian-styled, three-story 'showpiece' brewhouse and an irreverent, innovative approach to brewing that categorizes beers by flavor profiles like Session, Hop, and Smoke.
attractions.address: 401 E Main St (at Louisville Slugger Field)
attractions.hours: Sunday: 12:00 PM - 10:00 PM, Monday: 4:00 PM - 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 4:00 PM - 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 4:00 PM - 10:00 PM, Thursday: 4:00 PM - 10:00 PM, Friday: 12:00 PM - 11:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 PM - 11:00 PM (Tripadvisor)
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
attractions.admission: Free (Pay as you go for food and drinks)
highlights: "Victorian-styled, three-story 'showpiece' brewhouse with copper-clad vessels ([Against the Grain, Tripadvisor)", 'Located in a historic former train station with ornate industrial architecture (Tripadvisor, SABR)', 'Categorizes beer into six distinct styles: Session, Hop, Whim, Malt, Dark, and Smoke (Against the Grain)', 'Features a massive outdoor patio with views of Louisville Slugger Field (The Little Things Journal)', 'Smokehouse-inspired menu featuring signature items like Pork Belly on a Stick and griddle burgers (The Little Things Journal, Yelp)']
attractions.tips: Visit during a Louisville Bats home game to experience the energetic atmosphere of the stadium connection. Be sure to check the 'Whim' tap for limited-run experimental brews, and don't miss the chance to view the three-story brewhouse from the second-level fermentation area (The Little Things Journal, Pints Forks & Friends).

attractions.name: Akasha Brewing Company
attractions.description: Akasha Brewing Company is a craft brewery located in the heart of Louisville's NuLu district, specializing in innovative "funky" beers and sour flavors produced through wild fermentation. The taproom features a 24-tap draft wall with a wide variety of unfiltered and unpasteurized brews, ranging from light lagers to tart classics. It offers a relaxed, communal atmosphere with board games, arcade games, and a dog-friendly policy. GoToLouisville, Rick Lucas Blog

attractions.name: American Printing House for the Blind (Museum: The Dot Experience)
attractions.description: Founded in 1858, the American Printing House for the Blind is the world's largest nonprofit organization dedicated to creating educational, workplace, and lifestyle products for people who are blind or low vision. Its reimagined museum, The Dot Experience, is a transformative space that explores the authentic lived experiences and historical contributions of the visually impaired community through inclusive design and storytelling. American Printing House, The Dot Experience

attractions.hours: Daily: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM EST (Museum); APH Business Hours: Monday–Friday 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM The Dot Experience, APH
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed New Year’s Day, Derby Day, July 4, Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve, and Christmas Day) The Dot Experience

[The Dot Experience', 'APH Factory Tour – A behind-the-scenes look at the production of Braille books and accessible tools. The Dot Experience', 'Who We Are Exhibit – An exploration of the everyday realities and multifaceted identities of people living with vision loss. The Dot Experience', 'Change the World Gallery – A journey through the history of innovation, from Louis Braille’s invention to modern changemakers. The Dot Experience', 'Orientation Station – A dedicated space in the lobby to learn wayfinding tools like GoodMaps and tactile layouts. attractions.name: Angel's Envy Distillery (Angel's Envy official)
attractions.description: Angel's Envy Distillery is a family-run craft distillery located on Louisville's historic Whiskey Row, known for its unique secondary maturation process in port wine and rum barrels. Opened in 2016 as the first full-production whiskey distillery in downtown Louisville, it offers an intimate look at the distillation process within a beautifully restored early 20th-century complex (Visit All the Places, Whisky Advocate).
attractions.address: 500 E Main St, Louisville, KY 40202, U.S.A. (Angel's Envy official)
attractions.region: Downtown / Whiskey Row / NuLu (Angel's Envy official, Whisky Advocate)
attractions.hours: Monday–Tuesday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Wednesday–Thursday: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM; Friday–Saturday: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM; Sunday: 12:30 PM - 6:00 PM (Tripadvisor)
attractions.days_open: Open 7 days a week (Closed on certain holidays such as Jan 1, Jan 19, May 4, etc.) (Angel's Envy official)
attractions.admission: Signature Distillery Tour: $25–$31.80 (Tripadvisor, Whisky Advocate); Bottle Your Own Experience: Price varies (approx. $100+); Cocktail Classes: Approx. $40 (Angel's Envy Events); Under 21: Free (no tasting) (Tripadvisor)
highlights: 'Guided Signature Tour covering the entire production process from grain to bottle ([Viator)', 'Three-part bourbon tasting paired with locally crafted chocolates (Reddit, Whisky Advocate)', "Innovative 'Bottle Your Own' experience where guests can fill their own bottle of distillery-exclusive whiskey (Kentucky Bourbon Trail)", "The Finishing Room bar, offering seasonal handcrafted cocktails in a refined atmosphere (Angel's Envy Tours)", "Hands-on cocktail classes and sensory journeys through the distillery's fermentation and aging areas (Angel's Envy Tours)", "Located in a historic building with stunning architecture, including large 'angel's wings' doors (Reddit)"]
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended and can book up months in advance. Closed-toe shoes are required for distillery tours, and high heels are not permitted for safety. Visitors should arrive 15 minutes early; late entries are typically not accommodated. Guests under 21 are welcome on tours but cannot participate in tastings (Tripadvisor, Visit All the Places).

It offers more than 60 majors and is recognized for its small class sizes and personalized learning environment (Wikipedia, Bellarmine University).

attractions.name: Big Bat Bourbon (Louisville Slugger Museum Sister Attraction)
attractions.description: Big Bat Bourbon is a premier "sister attraction" to the Louisville Slugger Museum & Factory, offering an immersive hands-on bourbon blending experience. Visitors can craft, name, and bottle their own unique bourbon recipes in a space that celebrates Louisville's dual heritage of baseball and spirits (Big Bat Bourbon, GoToLouisville).

attractions.name: Big Four Bridge (Waterfront Park)
attractions.description: Originally built in 1895 as a railroad bridge, the Big Four Bridge now serves as a pedestrian and bicycle path connecting Louisville, Kentucky, to Jeffersonville, Indiana. It is a key feature of Louisville's Waterfront Park and is renowned for its spectacular LED lighting system that illuminates the Ohio River at night (Waterfront Park).
attractions.hours: Bridge: Open 24/7; Light Show: Nightly from dusk to 1 am (Waterfront Park FAQ)
attractions.admission: Free (Waterfront Park FAQ)
highlights: 'Connects Louisville, KY to Jeffersonville, IN across the Ohio River ([Waterfront Park)', 'Dynamic LED lighting system that wraps the iron fretwork in vibrant colors nightly (Waterfront Park)', 'Historic truss bridge originally constructed in 1895 for rail access (Waterfront Park)', 'Pedestrian and bicycle-only path providing safe river crossings (Waterfront Park FAQ)', 'Offers panoramic views of the Louisville skyline and the Ohio River (Tripadvisor)', 'Vietnam Memorial located at the base of the bridge ramp (Waterfront Park)']
attractions.tips: Pets are not allowed on the bridge (service animals only). The bridge may close during icy weather or the Thunder Over Louisville Air Show. Motorized vehicles, skateboards, and skates are prohibited (Waterfront Park FAQ).
attractions.nearby: Waterfront Park, PlayPort, Lincoln Memorial, Belle of Louisville, Joe's Crab Shack (Waterfront Park)

attractions.description: Butchertown is one of Louisville's oldest neighborhoods, originally serving as the city's meatpacking district in the 1800s. attractions.name: Cherokee Park
attractions.description: Designed in 1891 by Frederick Law Olmsted, Cherokee Park is a 409-acre municipal park that provides a pastoral setting featuring rolling hills, open meadows, and woodlands. It is a centerpiece of Louisville's Olmsted-designed park system and is famous for its 2.4-mile Scenic Loop, which offers dedicated lanes for both vehicular traffic and recreational users.
attractions.hours: 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM daily (Olmsted Parks Conservancy)
attractions.admission: Free (Olmsted Parks Conservancy)
highlights: ['2.4-mile Scenic Loop for walking, running, and cycling.', 'Big Rock picnic area and playground along Beargrass Creek.', "Hogan's Fountain, a historic landmark featuring a statue of the Greek god Pan.", 'Cherokee Golf Course, a 9-hole course that is the oldest continuously operating golf course in Kentucky.', 'Baringer Hill (also known as Dog Hill), a popular spot for dog owners and winter sledding.', 'Nettleroth Bird Sanctuary, a quiet area for birdwatching and nature observation.']
attractions.tips: The park has numerous entrances that can be confusing for first-time visitors; it is recommended to use a map. On the last Sunday of each month, 'Car-Free Cherokee' closes the park to vehicular traffic for pedestrians and cyclists. Parking is often available on the grass along the roads when designated spots are full. (Olmsted Parks Conservancy, Reddit)
attractions.nearby: Cave Hill Cemetery and Arboretum, Seneca Park, Bardstown Road shopping and dining district, and the historic Cherokee Triangle neighborhood. (Wikipedia, GoToLouisville)

Known for its iconic Twin Spires architecture, the venue serves as the first leg of the American Triple Crown and remains one of the world's most prestigious sporting locations. attractions.name: Copper & Kings American Brandy Company PR Newswire
attractions.description: Copper & Kings is a trailblazing American brandy company based in Louisville's historic Butchertown neighborhood, specializing in unadulterated pure pot-distilled spirits. Unlike traditional brandy producers, they age their products in bourbon barrels and utilize a unique "sonic aging" process where music is played to the barrels in their underground cellar. The distillery offers award-winning tours, a state-of-the-art rooftop bar with panoramic city views, and a focus on reclaiming brandy as a definitive American spirit. Copper & Kings, Distillery Trail

attractions.tips: Reservations for tours are highly recommended as they often sell out; don't miss the distillery-exclusive Mistelle dessert wine in the gift shop. Reddit

Located on the grounds of historic Churchill Downs, the museum features two floors of interactive exhibits, a 360-degree immersive film, and guided tours of the legendary racetrack Kentucky Derby Museum, Wikipedia.

attractions.description: Kentucky Peerless Distilling Company is a family-owned and operated craft distillery that revived a legacy dating back to 1889. Located in a restored 115-year-old building in downtown Louisville, the state-of-the-art facility handles the entire production process—milling, cooking, fermenting, distilling, barreling, and bottling—all under one roof Kentucky Peerless Distilling Co., GoToLouisville.

attractions.name: Kentucky Science Center
attractions.description: Kentucky's largest hands-on science museum is located on historic "Museum Row" in downtown Louisville's West Main District. It features three floors of interactive exhibits, a state-of-the-art four-story digital theater, and teaching laboratories designed to inspire curiosity in visitors of all ages (Official Website, Wikipedia). Through its "Do Science" philosophy, the center encourages guests to play, tinker, and explore the worlds of physics, biology, and engineering (Official Website).
attractions.hours: Daily: 9:30 AM – 5:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Exhibits: Adult $24, Child (2-12) $22, Members Free; Movie Only: Adult $7, Child $7, Members $5; Combo (Exhibit + Movie): Adult $29, Child $27, Members $5; Children under 2: Free (Official Website)
highlights: 'KidZone: A dedicated hands-on area for children 7 and under featuring a Splash Zone and water dynamics exploration ([Clio).', 'The World We Create: An interactive exhibit focusing on chemistry, physics, and engineering through hands-on building and testing (Clio).', 'Four-Story Digital Theater: An immersive cinema experience showing high-definition educational films from National Geographic and other sources (Official Website).', 'The World Within Us: A gallery exploring human health, medical careers, and the biological systems of the body (Clio).', 'The World Around Us: An exploration of natural sciences and ecology, including taxidermy displays and an authentic Egyptian mummy (Clio, Expedia).', "Discovery Gallery: Home to iconic items like the Gemini Trainer and polar bear specimens from the center's original collections (Official Website)."]
attractions.tips: At least one hour is recommended for self-guided exhibit exploration. Parking is available at 801 W. Washington St. for $15 daily, with a discounted rate of $8 for members. Visitors should check the daily schedule for live Tech Forum shows typically held at 2:30 PM (Official Website).

It serves as the official home for the University of Louisville men's and women's basketball teams and hosts a wide variety of high-profile concerts, family shows, and sporting events (KFC Yum! Center, Wikipedia). Opened in 2010, the $238 million facility has become a central hub for entertainment and economic revitalization in the region (Mortenson, PBS).

attractions.name: Louisville Ballet
attractions.description: Founded in 1952, Louisville Ballet is the fifth-oldest professional ballet company in the United States and the official state ballet of Kentucky. Led by Artistic Director Anthony Krutzkamp and CEO Leslie Smart, the company is dedicated to inspiring and educating the community through moving art and a diverse range of classical and contemporary performances.

attractions.name: Louisville Mega Cavern
attractions.description: Louisville Mega Cavern is a 100-acre man-made limestone cavern located 100 feet below the city of Louisville. Originally a 20th-century quarry, it has been transformed into an underground adventure park featuring zip lines, a ropes course, and historical tours. The facility maintains a constant year-round temperature of 58-60 degrees, making it a unique all-weather destination (Official Site).
attractions.hours: Mon-Fri: 9:00 AM - 8:30 PM, Sat-Sun: 9:30 AM - 8:30 PM (Courier Journal)
attractions.admission: Mega Zips: $99.99 per person, Mega Quest: $39.99 per person, Mega Tram: $19.99 per person, Mega Walking: $19.99 per person (Official Site)
highlights: "World's only fully underground zip line course (Mega Zips) ([Official Site)", "World's only fully underground aerial ropes challenge course (Mega Quest) (Official Site)", 'Historic 70-minute guided tram tour covering geology and mining history (Official Site)', 'Located 100 feet below the surface in a former limestone mine (PBS News)', 'Features a replica of a Cuban Missile Crisis fallout shelter (Official Site)', 'Constant 58-60 degree temperature year-round (Official Site)']
attractions.tips: Visitors should dress in layers or bring a jacket as the cavern maintains a constant 58-60 degree temperature. Closed-toed shoes are required for most activities. Reservations are strongly recommended as tours often sell out (Official Site). Please note that the Mega Underground Bike Park is currently closed indefinitely (Singletracks).
attractions.nearby: Louisville Zoo (located directly above the cavern), Joe Creason Park, Farmington Historic Plantation, Churchill Downs (TripAdvisor)

Today, it serves as a premier underground adventure destination, featuring the world's only fully underground zip line course, an aerial ropes challenge, and guided historical tours. Due to its support structures, it is officially classified as the largest building in the state of Kentucky (Official Site, Courier Journal)

The venue is renowned for its unique architectural integration of a historic 19th-century train shed and offers picturesque views of the Ohio River and the Louisville skyline (Wikipedia).

attractions.name: Old Forester Distilling Co. (Brown-Forman)
attractions.description: Old Forester Distilling Co. is the flagship distillery and visitor center for Brown-Forman’s founding brand, located in the heart of Louisville’s historic Whiskey Row. Housed in the same buildings the brand occupied from 1882 to 1919, the facility offers a complete "grain-to-glass" experience, including an on-site cooperage and urban rickhouse where bourbon is aged in the city center. It is the only place in downtown Louisville where visitors can witness live barrel making and charring alongside the distillation of Kentucky’s first bottled bourbon.

It consists of about 48 city blocks and is known for its high concentration of residential homes with stained glass windows and Victorian-era styles like Romanesque Revival and Queen Anne (Wikipedia). The neighborhood is also home to the renowned St. James Court and hosts the annual St. James Court Art Show (Old Louisville).

attractions.name: Oxmoor Farm (Bullitt Estate)
attractions.description: Oxmoor Farm is a historic 227-year-old estate that served as the home for six generations of the Bullitt family. Listed on the National Register of Historic Places, the 400-acre property features an original 1791 residence, a sprawling 10,000-book library, and beautifully landscaped gardens. Today, it operates as a venue for cultural events and offers specialized tours focusing on Kentucky's bourbon history and the family's legal legacy.

It is notable for being the only national cemetery to house a U.S. President's remains in the state of Kentucky (Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: Heaven Hill Bourbon Experience (formerly Heaven Hill Bourbon Heritage Center)
attractions.description: The Heaven Hill Bourbon Experience is an immersive visitor center located in the "Bourbon Capital of the World," offering interactive exhibits and guided tastings. It serves as an official stop on the Kentucky Bourbon Trail and features a state-of-the-art theater and specialized bottling experiences (Heaven Hill, Kentucky Bourbon Trail).
attractions.hours: Monday - Saturday: 9:30 a.m. - 5:00 p.m.; Sunday: 1:00 p.m. - 5:00 p.m. (Heaven Hill)
attractions.days_open: Daily (closed Easter, Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve/Day, and New Year's Day) (Heaven Hill)
attractions.admission: Magic of the Mashbill: $15.00; Whiskey Connoisseur: $20.00; You Do Bourbon: $40.00 (bottle cost additional); Heritage Rising: ~$60.00 (Heaven Hill, Reddit)
highlights: ['You Do Bourbon tasting and hands-on bottling experience', '1935 Distillery Theater featuring an immersive history show', 'Interactive galleries for brands like Elijah Craig and Larceny', 'Five Brothers Bar & Kitchen serving cocktails and deli fare', 'Shuttle tours to the cutting-edge Heaven Hill Springs Distillery', 'Extensive retail experience with Kentucky-exclusive whiskeys']
attractions.tips: It is highly recommended to book tour reservations online in advance as they frequently sell out. Arriving early (30-60 minutes) is advised for those hoping to secure limited bottle allocations, and visitors can receive a $2 tasting discount by subscribing to the official newsletter (Heaven Hill, Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: My Old Kentucky Home State Park, Oscar Getz Museum of Whiskey History, Willett Distillery, Lux Row Distillers (Visit Bardstown)

attractions.name: James G. Baker Center for Astronomy (Louisville Astronomical Society) Wikipedia
attractions.description: The James G. Baker Center for Astronomy is the flagship observatory of the Louisville Astronomical Society, featuring a state-of-the-art roll-off roof facility on 40 acres of dark-sky property. It houses powerful 16-inch and 14-inch telescopes used for both member research and monthly public star parties, while the Urban Astronomy Center in Louisville provides additional outreach and educational resources LAS News, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 3192 E. Curby Rd. Map to Curby
attractions.region: Crawford County, Indiana Map to Curby
attractions.hours: Public Star Parties: Monthly on Saturdays (typically around the New Moon) starting at dusk; Members have 24/7 access LAS Home, LAS Venues
attractions.days_open: Public star parties are held monthly on Saturdays, typically around the new moon LAS Home
attractions.admission: Free for public star parties; memberships are available for $50 per year Townepost, LAS Website
highlights: 'State-of-the-art roll-off roof observatory housing 16-inch Meade and 14-inch Celestron telescopes [LAS Venues', '40-acre dedicated dark-sky site for optimal deep-sky and galactic viewing LAS News', 'Climate-controlled multi-purpose building (T-2) with kitchen and classroom facilities LAS Venues', 'Monthly public star parties and telescope training workshops held from March through November LAS Home', 'Urban Astronomy Center located at E.P. "Tom" Sawyer State Park for convenient city-based outreach Wikipedia']
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 30 minutes before sunset to allow your eyes to adjust to the dark. Bring your own chairs, blankets, and bug spray, and always check the Clear Sky Chart on the official website for weather-related cancellations before traveling 360West, LAS Website

attractions.name: Mammoth Cave National Park
attractions.description: Mammoth Cave National Park preserves the world's longest known cave system, featuring over 426 miles of surveyed limestone passages. The park is a UNESCO World Heritage site and International Biosphere Reserve, encompassing the scenic Green River valley and lush Kentucky hills. Visitors can explore vast underground chambers, unique rock formations, and over 80 miles of surface trails. NPS Home, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 1 Visitor Center Parkway, Mammoth Cave, KY 42259-0007
attractions.hours: Park: Open 24 hours. Visitor Center (Summer 2026): 8:00 AM – 6:30 PM Daily. NPS Hours
attractions.admission: Park Entrance: Free. Cave Tours (Adult/Youth/Child): Mammoth Passage ($23/$17/$2), Domes & Dripstones ($28/$21/$2), Historic ($23/$17/$2), Frozen Niagara ($21/$16/$2), Grand Avenue ($38/$29/$2), Wild Cave ($79, 16+ only). Prices vary by tour; reservations recommended on Recreation.gov. NPS Fees, Recreation.gov
highlights: ["World's longest known cave system with over 426 miles explored.", 'Iconic formations like Frozen Niagara and the Mammoth Dome.', 'Historic pathways used by humans for over 5,000 years.', 'Over 80 miles of hiking trails and scenic river activities.', 'UNESCO World Heritage Site and International Biosphere Reserve.', 'Designated International Dark Sky Park for stargazing.']
attractions.tips: Book cave tours weeks in advance on Recreation.gov as they frequently sell out. Wear sturdy closed-toe shoes and bring a light jacket, as the cave stays a constant 54°F (12°C) year-round. Arrive at the visitor center at least 30 minutes before your scheduled tour time. NPS Basic Info
attractions.nearby: Abraham Lincoln Birthplace National Historical Park, Diamond Caverns, Bowling Green (National Corvette Museum), Dinosaur World, Cave City. NPS Nearby

attractions.name: Red Barn Visitor Center at Yew Dell Botanical Gardens
attractions.description: The Red Barn Visitor Center and Garden Gift Shop is the award-winning entrance to Yew Dell Botanical Gardens. Originally a historic tobacco barn, it was meticulously restored to serve as the gateway to this internationally-recognized horticultural site, offering visitors a unique blend of rustic heritage and modern architectural excellence. Yew Dell Botanical Gardens
attractions.hours: Tuesday - Saturday: 10 a.m. - 4 p.m.; Sunday: 12 p.m. - 4 p.m.; Monday: Closed. Yew Dell Botanical Gardens
attractions.admission: Adults: $12; Seniors 65+ & Youth 6-17: $8; Children 5 and under: Free; Members: Free; Veterans, Active & Retired Military with ID: Free. Yew Dell Botanical Gardens
